summary: Remote Filesystem not working on iOS

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: Godot 4.5.stable
- Not reproducible in: Godot 2.1.4, Godot 3.4

### System information

Godot 4.5 - iOS 26

### Issue description

Small Deploy with Remote Filesystem is not working on iOS, it prints to console the following log:

```
ERROR: Could not create directory: '/private/var/containers/Bundle/Application/A998D13C-9F82-492F-82F3-FF46214BB3CA/Test.app/remote_filesystem_files'.
at: make_dir_recursive (core/io/dir_access.cpp:180)
ERROR: Unable to open file for writing to remote filesystem cache: 'engine.cfg'.
at: _store_file (core/io/remote_filesystem_client.cpp:99)
ERROR: Unable to open the remote cache file for writing: '/private/var/containers/Bundle/Application/A998D13C-9F82-492F-82F3-FF46214BB3CA/Test.app/remote_filesystem.cache'.
at: _store_cache_file (core/io/remote_filesystem_client.cpp:121)
ERROR: Error writing the remote filesystem file cache.
at: _synchronize_with_server (core/io/remote_filesystem_client.cpp:323)
Could not connect to remotefs: <IP>:6010.
````

### Steps to reproduce

- Open a project in Godot, enable `Small Deploy with Remote Filesystem`
- Export iOS Project, open in Xcode and add the launch argument `--remote-fs <IP>`
- Run the app on an iOS device 
- Give the app Local Network access permission when asked (or you can find it in Settings > Privacy & Security > Local Network)

Any project will do, but here's one to test quickly

[RemoteFS.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22370643/RemoteFS.zip)

summary: Jolt Physics - Impacted performance when placing ShapeCast3Ds in the same global position as bodies' collision shapes

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.4.1.stable.mono, 4.5.stable.mono

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable.mono - macOS Sequoia (15.6.1) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M2 Max (Apple8) - Apple M2 Max (12 threads) - 32.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

Let's say I have 50 instances of a `StaticBody3D` scene that looks like this:
```
StaticBody3D
 └─ CollisionShape3D
```
where `StaticBody3D` is a `StaticBody3D`, and `CollisionShape3D` uses a `SphereShape3D` with a radius of 1 unit. `CollisionShape3D` is not transformed;

and 1 instance of a `ShapeCast3D` scene that looks like this:
```
ShapeCast3D
```
where `ShapeCast3D` uses a `SphereShape3D` with a radius of 5 units.

If I then place those 50 instances of `StaticBody3D`s and the `ShapeCast3D` instance in the same exact position (e.g., `Vector3.ZERO`) such that they overlap with one another, the fps will then plummet for some reason. But if the `ShapeCast3D` is translated even a little bit away from the `StaticBody3D` instances, the fps will return to normal.

Some observations:
- This issue seems to happen when using Jolt Physics only, since I can't reproduce it using the default physics engine.
- This issue only happens when the `StaticBody3D`s' `CollisionShape3D`'s global position (not the `StaticBody3D`s' global position) is the same as the `ShapeCast3D`'s global position.
- `CollisionShape3D`s placed as children under other `CollisionObject3D`-inherited classes can also lead to this issue.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Download the minimal reproduction project. All scripts are written in GDScript, so it can be run without a .NET version of Godot.
2. Open `main.tscn`, run the project. This will spawn 50 instances of `static_body.tscn` under the node `BodyParent`, and 1 instance of `shape_cast.tscn` under the node `ShapeCastParent`.
3. Click on the `reset` button in the running project, this sets both `BodyParent` and `ShapeCastParent` to the same global position (`Vector3.ZERO`). Take note of the fps, it should be very low.
4. Then, click on the `translated` button, this translates `ShapeCastParent` slightly to `Vector3(0.1, 0, 0)` so that it won't share the same global position as `BodyParent`. Take note of the fps as well, this time it should be normal.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22370406/mrp.zip)

summary: AudioServer.set_bus_volume_db doesn't work for non-Master buses

description.text:
### Tested versions

Exists: Godot 4.4, Godot 4.5

### System information

Godot version: 4.5 OS: MacOS (Apple M1 chip) Audio device: Built-in speakers/headphones

### Issue description

I'm experiencing an issue where AudioServer.set_bus_volume_db() doesn't actually change the volume for any audio buses other than "Master". The function executes without errors, but the volume remains unchanged.

Steps to reproduce:

Create a new audio bus (e.g., "Music") in Audio
Create a script that calls AudioServer.set_bus_volume_db() for this bus
Observe that the volume doesn't change, even though the function returns without errors
Code sample:

```gdscript

var music_bus_idx = AudioServer.get_bus_index("Music")
if music_bus_idx != -1:
    print("Before:", AudioServer.get_bus_volume_db(music_bus_idx))
    AudioServer.set_bus_volume_db(music_bus_idx, linear_to_db(0.5))  # Set to 50% volume
    print("After:", AudioServer.get_bus_volume_db(music_bus_idx))
    # Both print statements show the same value
```
Expected behavior:
The volume of the specified audio bus should change according to the value passed to set_bus_volume_db().

Actual behavior:
The volume remains unchanged, though the function doesn't report any errors. The issue occurs specifically on MacOS with M1 chip, but the "Master" bus works correctly.

Workaround:
Directly controlling the volume on individual AudioStreamPlayer nodes instead of using audio buses.

System information:

Godot version: 4.5
OS: MacOS (Apple M1 chip)
Audio device: Built-in speakers/headphones
Additional context:
I've verified that:

The audio buses are correctly created and named
No audio effects are applied to the buses
AudioStreamPlayer nodes are correctly assigned to the buses
The issue affects all non-Master buses
The problem appears to be in the audio server implementation for MacOS M1, specifically in how it handles volume changes for secondary audio buses.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a new audio bus (e.g., "Music") in Audio
Create a script that calls AudioServer.set_bus_volume_db() for this bus
Observe that the volume doesn't change, even though the function returns without errors

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

summary: _get_configuration_warning only runs for scene root on save, and icon doesn't disappear when warnings clear up.

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in Godot 4.4.1
- Reproducible in Godot 4.5


### System information

Operating System: Linux Mint 21.3, Kernel: Linux 5.15.0-153-generic

### Issue description

There's something odd happening with the _get_configuration_warnings method. It doesn't update as you'd expect. Sometimes the icon stays alive, while there are no warnings left. Sometimes the icon doesn't show up, while there should be warnings.

- _get_configuration_warnings runs for root nodes of a scene:
    - when opening / switching scenes in the editor.
    - when saving a scene in the editor
- _get_configuration_warnings only runs for child nodes of a scene:
   - when opening / switching scenes in the editor.
   - when the child node is added to the scene.
   - it does NOT run when saving the root scene in the editor.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new project
2. Create any type of Node
3. Attach this script to the root node.

```
@tool
extends Node

@export var show_warning: bool = true


func _get_configuration_warnings() -> PackedStringArray:
	print("Running _get_configuration_warnings in test_configuration_warnings.gd")
	var warnings := PackedStringArray()
	if show_warning:
		warnings.append("This is a configuration warning from test_configuration_warnings.gd")
	return warnings
```

4. Save the scene, and see the configuration warning pop up.
5. Uncheck the show_warning bool, save the scene, and see the configuration warning disappear.
6. Add a child node to the scene.
7. Add the same script to that child node.
8. See the configuration warning pop up.
9. Uncheck the show_warning bool, save the scene, and see that the configuration warning icon still shows.
10. Clicking the icon shows the print message in the console (proofing that _get_configuration_warning is still running, but no warning dialogue shows up, as the PackedStringArray is now empty.
11. Create a new scene. Switch to it.
12. Switch back to the old scene. The warning icon is now gone.
13. Check the show_warning bool again, and see how the warning icon does not pop up, nor does the error message print to the console.
14. Switch back to the new scene.
15. Switch back to the old scene.
16. Notice how the configuration warning icon is back again, and clicking it shows the error dialogue window.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[test_conf-warnings_godot45.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22365224/test_conf-warnings_godot45.zip)
[test_conf_warnings_godot441.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22365223/test_conf_warnings_godot441.zip)

summary: Abstract functions will have comments when they are completed during inheritance

description.text: ### Tested versions v4.5.stable.official [876b29033] ### System information Godot v4.5.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2060 with Max-Q Design (NVIDIA; 31.0.15.1700) - AMD Ryzen 7 4800H with Radeon Graphics (16 threads) - 23.37 GiB memory ### Issue description I created a class using abstract class keywords and added some abstract functions, then wrote comments for the functions. When I implemented the inheritance class of this class, I found that when completing abstract methods, I would insert the comments of the next function as completion content into the code. ### Steps to reproduce StateBase.gd ``` gdscript ## StateBase @abstract class_name StateBase extends Node var state_machine: StateMachine ## enter state func enter() -> void: pass ## exit state @abstract func exit() -> void @abstract func process_update(delta: float) -> void ## every physics process @abstract func physical_process_update(delta: float) -> void ``` Another code file: ``` gdscript extends StateBase func pr ``` At this point, a completion prompt will appear with comments for the parent class declaration file. If it is the `enter` method, it will not trigger the problem as it is not an abstract method. If it is the `exit` method, the absence of comments in the next function `process_update` will not trigger an issue. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [godot-test-4.5.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22359960/godot-test-4.5.zip)

summary: Error messages when running a project with a custom StyleBox in main theme

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.5.stable
- Not reproducible in 4.4.stable

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable (876b29033) - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - AMD Ryzen 9 5950X 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 31.92 GiB memory

### Issue description

When using a custom `StyleBox` (that is, a script that `extends StyleBox`) in the project's main theme that has an `@export` parameter, running the project prints the following error 8 times:
```
E 0:00:00:851   StyleBoxSomething.gd:5 @ @implicit_new(): Parameter "SceneTree::get_singleton()" is null.
  <C++ Source>  scene/debugger/scene_debugger.cpp:491 @ parse_message()
  <Stack Trace> StyleBoxSomething.gd:5 @ @implicit_new()
```

I only tested this using gdscript to make the custom style box. I did not try using C#.

### Steps to reproduce

In a new project:
1. Make a new script that `extends StyleBox`. Give it a `class_name`.
2. Add an `@export var` to the script. The type doesn't matter, and it doesn't matter if it's used at all.
3. Override the script's `_draw` method (an implementation of `pass` is fine).
4. Make a new theme file.
5. In the Project Settings, set `GUI/Theme/Custom` to point to the new theme file. This step is very important. This error will not reproduce if the theme is set explicitly or if using a Theme Override.

7. Add a new theme for a `Panel`.
8. Make its `panel` property (under StyleBoxes) use the new StyleBox.
9. Make a new scene file. Add a `Panel` as a child.
10. Run the scene. See the errors printed in the debugger panel.

The MRP should have all of this set up, and the one scene is set to be the main scene, so all you should need to do to reproduce is run the project.

summary: Fix Projection values being displayed transposed in the inspector

description.text:
In Godot's internal C++ code, the `[]` operator on Basis (3D) uses rows, but the `[]` operator on Projection uses columns. The inspector code in master was using the same numbers for Projection as it did for Basis, which resulted in the values being displayed as transposed. This PR fixes it by swapping the numbers, and I also threw in a change to preserve the unit just like it already does in Basis (3D) though this is different from Transform3D.

In 3D, the Basis inspector works correctly, as shown in this video:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a0e7d3b5-83f5-464f-947e-20c043b5003f

The Projection inspector is transposed in master, as shown in this video of it affecting the wrong 4D basis vector:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/15956305-b227-4d6f-b4a5-36e06106e82f

Simply put: the green-labeled values are supposed to move the green arrow, but the red arrow moved instead.

This PR fixes the issue, as shown in this video:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/95a03e7a-3221-4586-b866-15baf85b4480

This is purely a fix to the editor inspectors, there is no change to anywhere else.

summary: Scene inheritance node rename issue

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in Godot Engine v4.4.stable

### System information

Windows 10 - Godot Engine v4.4.stable.official.4c311cbee - https://godotengine.org OpenGL API OpenGL ES 3.0.0 (ANGLE 2.1.1 git hash: 468fcbe033fa) - Compatibility - Using Device: Google Inc. (Intel) - ANGLE (Intel, Intel(R) HD Graphics 5500 (0x00001616) Direct3D11 vs_5_0 ps_5_0, D3D11-20.19.15.5107)

### Issue description

When you rename a node in a parent scene that was used to inherit other scenes through scene instantiation. An error is generated if you updated the scene tree of the child scenes. A fair assumption is that the rename would've propagated in the child scenes as the symbols would've been updated, however this is not the case. Instead, an error is generated and the child's scene tree is updated with the node which was the child of the now renamed node, now, as a renamed orphaned node.


### Steps to reproduce

1. Create parent scene

> - **Action (Node)**
>   - Info (Node)
>   - Effects (Node)

2. Instance 2 child scenes through scene inheritance

> - **ChildA** & **ChildB**
>   - Info (Node)
>   - Effects (Node)

3. In ChildB, add new node (Poison) to the Effects node.

> - **ChildB**
>   - Info (Node)
>   - Effects (Node)
>     - Poison (Node) 

		
4. Rename the Effects node in the parent EnemyEffects

> - **Action (Node)**
>   - Info (Node)
>   - EnemyEffects (Node)

5. In ChildA scene, the node is updated correctly. 

> - **ChildA**
>   - Info (Node)
>   - EnemyEffects (Node)

However, in ChildB there is an error.

> - **ChildB**
>   - **Effects#Poison (Node)**
>   - Info (Node)
>   - EnemyEffects (Node)

**Errors**
- The node was renamed however, the child node was removed
- The child node of the prior Effects node was orphaned and its name was updated to its previous parent
- Log Messages
  - ERROR: Node not found: "Effects/Poison" (relative to "ChildB").
  - WARNING: scene/resources/packed_scene.cpp:178 - Parent path 'EnemyEffects' for node 'Poison' has vanished when instantiating: ''.

summary: EditorSpinSlider enhancement and bug fixes.

description.text:
Changes:
* Fix: Slider background size.
* Add: New hint `prefer_arrows` for float editing, complementing the existing `prefer_slider` hint for integers.
* Full RTL layout support.
* Fix scaling issues for slider grabber.
* Make sure text draw position always matches the `LineEdit` text position.
* Fix unable to move the mouse after changing the value with mouse wheel up/down while grabbing the slider grabber.
* Add up and down icons similar to SpinBox and allow right clicking them to set value to max/min.
* Remove an extra `Control` node used as the parent of the internal `LineEdit` which is no longer needed.
* Added proper theme cache properties instead of using get_theme* inside the class.
* Refactor: Removed the `control_state` property (which previously controlled slider/arrow visibility and the integer-only `prefer_slider` flag). Introduced clearer, more descriptive properties, `hide_control`, `integer_prefer_slider` and `float_prefer_arrows`. Note: `ControlState` remains internally to tell if we are currently using slider or arrows for both floats and integers.
* <s> Add symmetric fill support, useful for Control.rotation property. </s> Will be added in another PR following this.

summary: Add 3D arcball rotation mode implementation

This PR adds an arcball style rotation, similar to the one in Unreal engine. This allows for a more natural feel when freely rotating an object in 3D. The arcball rotation is added as an editor preference, so this default 3D behavior style is still available for those who want it.

summary: Duplicating `ShaderMaterial` reverts certain shader parameters

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in v4.5.rc.double.custom_build [bfa330dd5]

Did not test in any other version yet.

### System information

Godot v4.5.rc.double (bfa330dd5) - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8129) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-12400F (12 threads) - 31.84 GiB memory

### Issue description

Duplicating a ShaderMaterial with modified shader parameters that were set by code (not in the editor) will reset certain types back to the "default" (editor) values for both the original and the duplicated ShaderMaterial.

For example, if you set a shader parameter to be a certain Vector2, and then duplicate the material, the original and the duplicate materials with both now revert this Vector2 to the one that can be seen in the editor, i.e. the parameter that was just set is gone, even for the original material!

Simple example: Editor shows Vector2(0,0) for some parameter. You change it to Vector2(1, 1) in code (`set_shader_parameter`), and after duplicating this one material, all instances of that same material will now show Vector2(0,0) again.

I can reproduce this for Vector2 and Vector3 types, but not for float and int for example.

### Steps to reproduce

Open the MRP and click play, in the console output you will see the originally set parameter and then the values after `mat_a.duplicate()` has been called.

The editor shows Vector2(0,0) for albedo_texture_size. Changing it to Vector2.ONE and printing the parameter is successfull. But after duplicating, the value reverts back to Vector2(0,0). The same is NOT true for the float point_size for example.

[min-repro-shader.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22314547/min-repro-shader.zip)

summary: Imported AnimationLibrary read-only handling is incorrect after adding extra AnimationLibrary into AnimationMixer

description.text: ### Tested versions - Reproducible 4.1, 4.2, 4.3, 4.4, 4.5 ### System information Any ### Issue description Imported Animations should not be edited, as their state reverts each time they are re-imported. The default Global (empty string) AnimationLibrary immediately after import correctly handles read-only. However, adding an AnimationLibrary to the AnimationMixer containing it breaks the read-only handling. This changes its state from "foreign" to "built-in", making it editable. Although the track has a separate imported flag that triggers the warning below, all operations are permitted in the GUI. Looking at the source code, it appears to determine this by checking whether `.import` exists in the resource path's filesystem. However, since the path changed after adding the AnimationLibrary, the determination seems to be failing. This issue has existed since version 4.1, prior to the AnimationMixer implementation, so it likely occured with the initial implementation of the AnimationLibrary. Disabling the ManageAnimationLibrary option for imported AnimationPlayers is the simplest solution, but I'm concerned about breaking compatibility. Another potential fix involves preserving the entity paths of Animations within the modified AnimationLibraryList, though I'm unsure if this is feasible. ### Steps to reproduce Add the AnimationLibrary to the imported AnimationMixer, save the .tscn file, and reopen it. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [import_animlib.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22309417/import_animlib.zip)

summary: Strange scaling in StyleBoxTexture/NinePatchRect if DPITexture was loaded with content_scale_factor != 1

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible after #109118

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta6 - Arch Linux #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu, 11 Sep 2025 17:42:36 +0000 on Wayland - X11 display driver, Single-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Mesa Intel(R) Graphics (ADL GT2) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-1235U (12 threads) - 15.35 ГиБ memory

### Issue description

If DPITexture was rasterized when content scale factor wasn't equal to 1 (or in canvas item scaling mode when window size wasn't equal content size), when used in NinePatchRect, StyleBoxTexture or similar it will be working not very good (see video below). Resetting content scale factor to 1 manually or via window resize fixes issue.
Also, if there is any usage except specified (NinePatchRect or StyleBoxTexture), problem is gone

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dce0c876-cdfb-4198-ad01-9fdfe39bb80a

### Steps to reproduce

1. Add DPITexture to StyleBoxTexture or NinePatchRect (don't add to anything else!)
2. Set Content Scale Factor to something greater than 1.0
3. Run the game, notice texture doesn't get oversampled to specified scale factor
4. Try to change window size, notice oversampling working differently

summary: _ready called twice on custom bottom_panel controls

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.5.rc2, 4.4.1.stable

### System information

Windows 11, Godot Engine v4.5.rc2.officia, Forward+ - dedicated geforce 4080 gtx

### Issue description

Controls added with [EditorPlugin.add_control_to_bottom_panel](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/4.4/classes/class_editorplugin.html#class-editorplugin-method-add-control-to-bottom-panel) in an addons `enter_tree` function have their `_ready` function called twice.

This is a problem since bottom panels often connect signals in their _ready and if signals are connected multiple times it can result in undesired behaviour.

Note that `@onready` calls are also affected.

### Steps to reproduce

- Create an addon with the following:
```gdscript
# myplugin.gd
@tool
extends EditorPlugin

var mypanel: MyPanel

func _enter_tree() -> void:
	print("entering tree")
	mypanel = load("res://addons/bottompaneltest/my_panel.tscn").instantiate()
	add_control_to_bottom_panel(mypanel, "My Panel")


func _exit_tree() -> void:
	remove_control_from_bottom_panel(mypanel)

# my_panel.gd
@tool
extends Control
class_name MyPanel

func _ready() -> void:
	print("_ready called")

```
- Restart the editor

You'll see the following output:
> entering tree
_ready called
_ready called


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

MRP: [bottom-panel-test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22308951/bottom-panel-test.zip)

summary: RDShaderFile not updating when edited in external editor, despite explicit load with ResourceLoader.CACHE_MODE_IGNORE

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducable in 4.5-rc2, 4.4.1

### System information

Godot v4.5.rc2 - macOS Sonoma (14.7.8) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M3 Max (Apple9) - Apple M3 Max (16 threads) - 64.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

I'm working on a glsl shader used in a CompositorEffect, and want to hot-reload the shader as I make changes in an eternal editor.  I got all the code working, only to discover, the GLSL is not reloaded unless I explicitly switch back to the godot editor, despite telling ResourceLoader to ignore the cache:

```gdscript
    var shader_file: RDShaderFile = ResourceLoader.load(glsl_shader_file, "", ResourceLoader.CACHE_MODE_IGNORE)
```

When I save changes to the glsl file, my `_reload_shader()` compiling the shader and recreating the pipeline, but it doesn't visually update.  But, if I click over to the Godot Editor, I can see it reload the shader, then go back to my editor and save again, the shader updates.

Also, the updated shader is not the most recent version on disk; it's the one that was loaded when I clicked over into the godot editor.

You can see this happen in the video below.  I start by changing the color of the triangle from blue to green in the shader.  When I save the shader, the console shows the shader and pipeline are rebuilt, but the triangle stays blue on screen.  I then click over to godot, then back to my editor, and set the triangle color to red, and save to disk.  At this time, the pipeline rebuilds, and the visual changes to show **the green** triangle; which was the state it was in the last time I clicked on the editor.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/363c8ae6-691f-49c8-97f0-154420080210

Is this the intended behavior?  Is there a way to disable the caching so I can get hot-reload working?

### Steps to reproduce

* Start the attached project
* In an external editor make a change to outline.glsl, and save the file
* Note that the change does not appear in the game window
* Click over to the godot editor
* Switch back to external editor and save again
* Note that the change appears in the game window

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Project is here: [rd_shader_file-caching_issue.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22305036/rd_shader_file-caching_issue.zip)

It's minimal-ish because I didn't want to have to rebuild the whole example outside of a CompositorEffect.  The key function is in `outline_compositor_effect.gd` to reload the shader.  It is triggered by the scene root `_process()` detecting that the shader file has changed.

```gdscript
func _reload_shader():
    print("rebuilding shader")

    # destroy old shader if present
    if shader.is_valid():
        rd.free_rid(shader)
        shader = RID()
        pipeline = RID()


    # load the shader
    # XXX The issue is here: if you edit the shader in an external editor, the
    #     changes are not loaded, despite ResourceLoader.CACHE_MODE_IGNORE.
    #     You have to switch into a godot window and resave before the most
    #     recent version is loaded.
    var shader_file: RDShaderFile = ResourceLoader.load(glsl_shader_file, "", ResourceLoader.CACHE_MODE_IGNORE)
    var shader_spirv: RDShaderSPIRV = shader_file.get_spirv()
    if shader_file.base_error != "":
        push_error(shader_file.base_error)
        return
    if shader_spirv.compile_error_vertex != "":
        push_error(shader_spirv.compile_error_vertex)
        return
    if shader_spirv.compile_error_fragment != "":
        push_error(shader_spirv.compile_error_fragment)
        return
    shader = rd.shader_create_from_spirv(shader_spirv)

    #create the pipeline
    var blend := RDPipelineColorBlendState.new()
    var blend_attachment := RDPipelineColorBlendStateAttachment.new()
    blend.attachments.push_back(blend_attachment)
    pipeline = rd.render_pipeline_create(
        shader,
        rd.screen_get_framebuffer_format(),
        vertex_format,
        RenderingDevice.RENDER_PRIMITIVE_TRIANGLES,
        RDPipelineRasterizationState.new(),
        RDPipelineMultisampleState.new(),
        RDPipelineDepthStencilState.new(),
        blend
    )
    print("new pipeline ", pipeline)
```

summary: Allow to use sliders for integers in `EditorSpinSlider`

description.text:
- Deprecate `hide_slider` in favor of `control_state`. This takes an enum that allows for prefer using a slider, use the default for the value, or hide the controls.
- Dragging the value in integers if the slider is used, with the speed of which it changes being affected by the new `interface/inspector/integer_drag_speed` editor setting.
- Stop truncating float values to one decimal.
- Make the slider grabber always stay highlight if holding it, even if the mouse leaves it.
- Fix tooltip value for integers being shown as floats.

Closes #42809.
